instruction:
Kelvin the Frog calls a $5$ -digit integer $\overline{d_1d_2d_3d_4d_5}$ " cool beans" if each of the $d_i$ are nonzero and pairwise distinct (e.g. no two digits are the same), $d_1 < d_2 < d_3$ and $d_3 > d_4 > d_5$ . For example, the integer $18932$ is cool beans, but $13931$ and $16798$ are not. How many $5$ -digit integers are cool beans?

Options:
A. 756
B. 729
C. 702
D. 735
E. 750
F. 768
G. 720
H. 744
I. 760
J. 740
